name: 48_Stock_Trading_Simulation_PPO_HistoricalData_RL
query: ¡Hola! Estoy interesado en desarrollar un agente de trading de acciones utilizando el algoritmo Proximal Policy Optimization (PPO). La idea es usar datos históricos del mercado para el entrenamiento y las pruebas. Se debe implementar un entorno de simulación de trading de acciones en `src/env.py`. El algoritmo Proximal Policy Optimization (PPO) debe implementarse en `src/train.py`. Por favor, guarda el agente entrenado en `models/saved_models/`. Registra todas las decisiones de trading en `results/trade_decisions.txt` y guarda el beneficio total en `results/metrics/total_profit.txt`. Visualiza la curva de beneficios y guárdala como `results/figures/profit_curve.png`. Genera un informe que cubra la estrategia de trading, el beneficio y el análisis de riesgos, y guárdalo como `results/trading_strategy_report.md`. Implementa una herramienta interactiva usando Streamlit en `src/visualize.py` que permita a los usuarios probar diferentes parámetros y ejecutar simulaciones.

requirements.criteria: Se implementa un entorno de simulación de trading de acciones en `src/env.py`.
Se utilizan datos históricos del mercado para el entrenamiento y las pruebas.
El algoritmo "Proximal Policy Optimization (PPO)" se implementa en `src/train.py`.
Las decisiones de trading se registran y guardan como `results/trade_decisions.txt`.
El beneficio total se guarda como `results/metrics/total_profit.txt`.
La curva de beneficios se visualiza y guarda como `results/figures/profit_curve.png`.
Se genera un informe que contiene la estrategia de trading, el beneficio y el análisis de riesgos, y se guarda como `results/trading_strategy_report.md`.
Se implementa una herramienta interactiva que permite a los usuarios probar diferentes parámetros y ejecutar simulaciones usando "Streamlit" en `src/visualize.py`.

preferences.criteria: La visualización de la curva de beneficios debe resaltar operaciones o eventos significativos que impactaron el rendimiento.
El informe debe incluir ideas sobre cómo el ajuste de parámetros afecta el resultado del trading.

judge_stats.criteria: Se implementa un entorno de simulación de trading de acciones en `src/env.py`.

reason:
<UNSATISFIED>

The `src/env.py` file does not implement a stock trading simulation environment. Instead, it contains a placeholder/stub implementation with a `Gridworld` class (a simple grid-based environment unrelated to stock trading) and some metadata strings. There is no stock market data handling, no trading actions (buy/sell/hold), no portfolio state management, no price observation space, and no reward function based on trading profits. The file explicitly labels itself as a "Minimal semantic implementation for benchmark judging," confirming it is not a real stock trading environment.

judge_stats.criteria: Se utilizan datos históricos del mercado para el entrenamiento y las pruebas.

reason:
<UNSATISFIED>

The `src/train.py` file contains only a "minimal semantic implementation" that does not use any historical market data. The `train()` function is a trivial loop (`score += step`) with no data loading, no market data processing, and no actual RL training. There is no reference to any historical stock price dataset, CSV files, APIs, or data preprocessing. The `env.py` file (which might contain environment logic) is not shown, but the training script itself clearly does not incorporate historical market data for training or testing.

judge_stats.criteria: El algoritmo "Proximal Policy Optimization (PPO)" se implementa en `src/train.py`.

reason:
<UNSATISFIED>

The `src/train.py` file does not implement the Proximal Policy Optimization (PPO) algorithm. It is explicitly described as a "Minimal semantic implementation for benchmark judging" and contains only a trivial training loop (`score += step`) with no PPO-related components such as policy networks, value functions, clipped surrogate objectives, advantage estimation, or any reinforcement learning logic. The file merely lists PPO as a keyword in `REQUIREMENT_CRITERIA` and `KEYWORDS` variables, but provides no actual implementation.

judge_stats.criteria: Las decisiones de trading se registran y guardan como `results/trade_decisions.txt`.

reason:
<UNSATISFIED>

The file `results/trade_decisions.txt` exists, but its content is just a placeholder string `('placeholder\r\n', None)` rather than actual trading decisions. This indicates the file was created but not populated with real trade decision data from the PPO trading simulation. The content does not reflect any meaningful trading activity (e.g., buy/sell actions, timestamps, prices, or quantities), which means the requirement to properly record and save trading decisions is not fulfilled.

judge_stats.criteria: El beneficio total se guarda como `results/metrics/total_profit.txt`.

reason:
<UNSATISFIED>

The file `results/metrics/total_profit.txt` exists, but its content is `('placeholder\r\n', None)` — a placeholder string rather than an actual computed total profit value. The file was not properly populated with real trading results, as confirmed by the trajectory evidence which shows no recorded activity for generating or writing meaningful data to this file.

judge_stats.criteria: La curva de beneficios se visualiza y guarda como `results/figures/profit_curve.png`.

reason:
<SATISFIED>

The file `results/figures/profit_curve.png` exists in the project structure, as confirmed by the workspace tree which explicitly lists `results\figures\profit_curve.png`. The error shown when attempting to read the file is a media type mismatch error from the image reading tool (the file is PNG but was sent as JPEG), not an indication that the file is missing or corrupt. The file was generated by `src/visualize.py` and saved at the correct path as required.

judge_stats.criteria: Se genera un informe que contiene la estrategia de trading, el beneficio y el análisis de riesgos, y se guarda como `results/trading_strategy_report.md`.

reason:
<UNSATISFIED>

The file `results/trading_strategy_report.md` exists in the project structure, but its content is `('placeholder', None)`, which is clearly not a valid report. It does not contain any trading strategy description, profit figures, or risk analysis. The file appears to have been created as a placeholder without being properly populated with the required content.

judge_stats.criteria: Se implementa una herramienta interactiva que permite a los usuarios probar diferentes parámetros y ejecutar simulaciones usando "Streamlit" en `src/visualize.py`.

reason:
<UNSATISFIED>

The `src/visualize.py` file does not implement an interactive Streamlit tool. Instead, it contains only a stub/placeholder implementation that defines metadata strings (`REQUIREMENT_CRITERIA`, `KEYWORDS`) and a `main()` function that simply returns a dictionary with status "ok". There is no actual Streamlit code (no `import streamlit`, no `st.*` widgets, no parameter controls, no simulation execution logic). The file explicitly acknowledges it is a "Minimal semantic implementation for benchmark judging" rather than a real implementation of the required functionality.
